hyperedges.label: Unsupervised physics-guided FWI: three approaches sharing the pattern of (1) no labeled velocity-seismic pairs, (2) physics constraint replaces supervision, (3) CNN-based architecture
OpenFWI benchmark ecosystem: dataset (A1), review contextualizing it (A2), and scaling study on it (A4) form a coherent research platform for data-driven FWI
PGML-FWI taxonomy: Lin et al. 2023 review categorizes A3 (PINN), A5 (autoencoder FWI), and UPFWI all as physics-guided approaches under a unified six-category framework
ML-Based Velocity Model Building Pipeline: FWI + ML supervision + Physical constraints
GAN-Based Seismic Data Processing: Ground-Roll Attenuation, Trace Interpolation, CycleGAN Architecture
Bayesian Framework for Seismic Inversion with Uncertainty: SBI + Diffusion Networks + Physics Summary Statistics
D3 covers all major seismic inversion method families
Uncertainty quantification methods for seismic inversion
Geochemical proxies used to reconstruct Ediacaran paleoenvironment in D2
Machine learning approaches for seismic inversion

nodes.label: Reflection residual statics estimated using a high-resolution neural network

attributes.affiliation: University of Science and Technology of China, Geophysical Research Institute, School of Earth and Space Sciences, Hefei, China
attributes.rationale: Title, authors, journal, volume, pages, DOI and dates explicitly stated on first page of paper

nodes.norm_label: reflection residual statics estimated using a high-resolution neural network

attributes.affiliation: University of Science and Technology of China
attributes.rationale: Explicitly listed as first author with email and affiliation on title page

attributes.affiliation: University of Science and Technology of China
attributes.rationale: Explicitly listed as corresponding author with email and affiliation on title page

attributes.description: Correction of short- to medium-wavelength static time shifts in seismic traces associated with near-surface irregularities; applied after long-wavelength statics correction to improve seismic imaging
attributes.conventional_approach: stack power maximization with NMO correction
attributes.rationale: Central topic of the paper, explicitly defined and discussed throughout

attributes.description: Neural network backbone that maintains high-resolution representations throughout the entire forward pass by using parallel convolutions at multiple resolutions with multiresolution fusion modules; adapted here for seismic residual statics prediction
attributes.original_authors: Sun et al. 2019; Wang et al. 2020
attributes.modifications_in_this_work: stride-2 convolution applied only in time direction; batch normalization replaced by group normalization
attributes.rationale: Explicitly described as the backbone architecture with architectural details provided

nodes.label: Deep Learning Residual Statics Estimation Method

attributes.description: CNN-based method using HRNet backbone to predict surface-consistent residual statics directly from common-shot, common-receiver, or CMP gathers without NMO correction or velocity analysis
attributes.prediction_head: multiscale training and multiscale testing via adaptive average pooling
attributes.rationale: Proposed method of the paper, fully described in METHOD section

nodes.norm_label: deep learning residual statics estimation method

attributes.description: Generates large numbers of training samples by applying synthetic random surface-consistent short- to medium-wavelength statics to existing gathers; augmented with random noise, polarity reversal, horizontal flipping, and cropping
attributes.rationale_in_paper: Addresses scarcity of labeled real seismic data for training
attributes.synthetic_statics_distribution: band-pass filtered Gaussian random numbers, rescaled to -1 to 1, scaled by Gaussian-sampled multiplier (mean 10ms, std 5ms)
attributes.rationale: Explicitly described in 'Data preparation and augmentation' section

attributes.description: Conventional gold-standard method for estimating surface-consistent residual statics by maximizing stack power over NMO-corrected CMP gathers; requires NMO velocity picking and assumes hyperbolic moveout
attributes.limitation: Requires NMO velocity analysis; breaks down under large lateral velocity variations
attributes.rationale: Described and compared against throughout the paper as the established baseline method

attributes.description: Seismic processing step that corrects for the travel-time difference between offset traces and zero-offset traces assuming hyperbolic moveout; prerequisite for conventional stack power residual statics methods
attributes.rationale: Repeatedly discussed as a prerequisite for conventional statics methods and an assumption the proposed method avoids

attributes.description: Normalization technique that divides channels into groups and normalizes within each group; used instead of batch normalization due to small batch size constraints on GPU
attributes.rationale: Explicitly selected over batch normalization and explained in architecture section

attributes.description: Encoder-decoder convolutional network that recovers high-resolution representations from low-resolution by upsampling; used as baseline comparison in robustness tests
attributes.comparison_result: Lower generalization ability than HRNet on validation set; higher residual statics error at low S/N
attributes.rationale: Explicitly compared with HRNet in robustness tests section

attributes.description: Long-wavelength statics correction derived from near-surface velocity model obtained by first-arrival traveltime tomography; applied before residual statics correction
attributes.rationale: Explicitly described as preprocessing step in all real data applications

attributes.description: Seismic imaging method applied to evaluate the quality of statics corrections in the third real test dataset
attributes.rationale: Explicitly mentioned and migration sections shown for third real data test

attributes.description: 64,000 seismic gather samples generated from 8 velocity models using forward acoustic modeling; synthetic statics applied; 20% used for validation
attributes.rationale: Explicitly described in 'Training with synthetic data' section

attributes.description: 102,090 labeled samples generated from two land seismic surveys; statics-free reflections obtained via tomostatics and stack power maximization; random synthetic statics then applied
attributes.rationale: Explicitly described in 'Training with real data' section; providers acknowledged

attributes.description: Three real seismic datasets from new exploration areas used to evaluate the trained models
attributes.dataset_1: 467 shot gathers, max offset 3000m, receiver interval 10m
attributes.dataset_2: 1243 shot gathers, max offset 6850ft, receiver interval 55ft
attributes.dataset_3: 194 shot gathers, max offset 5000m, receiver interval 30m, rugged topography with large lateral velocity variations
attributes.rationale: Described in 'Real applications' section with survey geometry details

attributes.description: Loss function combining L2 behavior for small errors and L1 behavior for large errors; shown to outperform L2 norm for regression in deep learning
attributes.rationale: Explicitly defined as Equation 1 and 2 in the paper

attributes.description: Assumption that residual statics are associated with shot and receiver surface locations, causing a constant time shift along an entire trace; underpins both conventional and DL-based statics methods
attributes.limitation: Breaks down when near-surface velocity is higher than bedrock velocity
attributes.rationale: Identified as the most important assumption for residual statics correction; discussed in both methodology and discussion sections

attributes.description: Deep learning architecture using residual modules; used as the convolution unit inside each stage of HRNet
attributes.rationale: Explicitly cited as the residual module used within HRNet stages

attributes.description: DL training with fully labelled datasets; uses error-based feedback to optimize parameters

links.note: D2 is Ediacaran geochemistry/paleontology; D3/D4 are seismic geophysics. Despite being in the same cluster folder, the papers have no thematic overlap.
D4 file content is byte-for-byte identical to D3; both contain Hosseinzadeh et al. 2025.
Pulsed oxygenation events discussed in the paper are the mechanism underlying the Neoproterozoic Oxygenation Event concept.
Both BLI and CI are post-stack methods that compute acoustic impedance by integrating reflectivity with spectral correction; CI adds spectral shaping.
Both FWI and CNN-based inversion aim at high-resolution subsurface modelling from full seismic waveforms; CNN can approximate FWI at reduced computational cost.
Both CNN and DNN are ML-based inversion approaches that learn seismic-to-property mappings; CNN focuses on spatial patterns, DNN on elastic intermediate representation.
Both Bayesian inversion and EnKF address uncertainty quantification in subsurface property estimation; they solve the same problem with different statistical frameworks.

attributes.rationale: Authors claim InvNet3D is the first deep-learning-based solution for 3D FWI to the best of their knowledge

attributes.note: Cited as evidence that deeper networks are better feature extractors
Used at conv_7 layer with group size 512
Similar approach of partial reversibility; both reduce memory by avoiding storing all activations
Implementation based on additive coupling as in NICE

attributes.rationale: Paper states invertible networks make a step further than gradient checkpointing by enabling reconstruction of input at all invertible layers
MobileNets cited as a key reference for group/depthwise convolution techniques used in the paper
Authors propose a similar large-scale open dataset is needed for seismic fault interpretation

hyperedges.label: Unsupervised physics-guided FWI: three approaches sharing the pattern of (1) no labeled velocity-seismic pairs, (2) physics constraint replaces supervision, (3) CNN-based architecture
OpenFWI benchmark ecosystem: dataset (A1), review contextualizing it (A2), and scaling study on it (A4) form a coherent research platform for data-driven FWI
PGML-FWI taxonomy: Lin et al. 2023 review categorizes A3 (PINN), A5 (autoencoder FWI), and UPFWI all as physics-guided approaches under a unified six-category framework
ML-Based Velocity Model Building Pipeline: FWI + ML supervision + Physical constraints
GAN-Based Seismic Data Processing: Ground-Roll Attenuation, Trace Interpolation, CycleGAN Architecture
Bayesian Framework for Seismic Inversion with Uncertainty: SBI + Diffusion Networks + Physics Summary Statistics
D3 covers all major seismic inversion method families
Uncertainty quantification methods for seismic inversion
Geochemical proxies used to reconstruct Ediacaran paleoenvironment in D2
Machine learning approaches for seismic inversion
